scope: global corridor/route identity cleanup; prepared aliases are route-only because the safest remaining high-confidence candidates were route records

proposed_aliases.reason: Same global route: both records are Bx41 local service on Webster Avenue. The target is the broader Bx41 Local record with many source links; the alias is a one-source November 2012 Webster Avenue instance with the same route_id and local service variant.
Same global route: both records identify Bx55 Limited service in the Webster Avenue/Bx41 SBS planning context. Route ID and limited-stop service variant match exactly.
Same global route: both records are BxM6 express bus service. The alias is a Madison Avenue CB6 June 2025 study-area mention; the target is the broader BxM6 Express Bus record.
Same global route: both records are BxM7 express bus service. The alias is a Madison Avenue CB6 June 2025 study-area mention; the target is the broader BxM7 Express Bus record.
Same global route: both records identify Q101 local service in Queensboro Bridge study evidence. Route ID and local service variant match exactly.
Same global route: both records identify QM10 express service in Queensboro Bridge study evidence. Route ID and express service variant match exactly.
Same global route: both records identify QM12 express service in Queensboro Bridge study evidence. Route ID and express service variant match exactly.
Same global route: both records identify QM18 express service in Queensboro Bridge study evidence. Route ID and express service variant match exactly.

candidate_discovery.route_group_command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select upper(coalesce(routes.route_id,'')) as route_id, coalesce(json_extract(records.payload,'$.service_variant'),'') as service_variant, coalesce(routes.route_type_normalized,'') as route_type_normalized, count(*) as n, group_concat(records.record_id, ' | ') as ids from records join routes on records.record_id=routes.record_id where records.record_kind='route' and coalesce(routes.route_id,'') != '' group by upper(routes.route_id), coalesce(json_extract(records.payload,'$.service_variant'),''), coalesce(routes.route_type_normalized,'') having n > 1 order by n desc, route_id limit 80;"
candidate_discovery.corridor_group_command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select lower(coalesce(corridors.corridor_name, corridors.name, corridors.street, records.display_name,'')) as name_key, count(*) as n, group_concat(records.record_id, ' | ') as ids from records join corridors on records.record_id=corridors.record_id where records.record_kind='corridor' and coalesce(corridors.corridor_name, corridors.name, corridors.street, records.display_name,'') != '' group by lower(coalesce(corridors.corridor_name, corridors.name, corridors.street, records.display_name,'')) having n > 1 order by n desc, name_key limit 100;"
candidate_discovery.result_summary: Route duplicate groups provided the safest remaining candidates with exact route_id and service-variant agreement. Corridor duplicate groups were inspected but not selected because remaining same-name clusters had materially different or ambiguous limits/scope risk.

record_existence_and_kind.command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select r.record_id, r.record_kind, r.display_name, routes.route_id, json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant') as service_variant, routes.route_type_normalized, routes.route_name, routes.route_label, r.primary_source_id, (select count(*) from record_sources rs where rs.record_id=r.record_id) as source_count from records r join routes on r.record_id=routes.record_id where r.record_id in ('route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster','route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1','route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster','route_bx55-limited-webster-2012','route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm6','route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm7','route_q101-201110-qbb','route_q101-qbb-study','route_qm10-201110-qbb','route_qm10-qbb-study','route_qm12-201110-qbb','route_qm12-qbb-study','route_qm18-201110-qbb','route_qm18-qbb-study') order by r.record_id;"
record_existence_and_kind.count_command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select count(*) as selected_record_count from records where record_id in ('route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster','route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1','route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster','route_bx55-limited-webster-2012','route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm6','route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm7','route_q101-201110-qbb','route_q101-qbb-study','route_qm10-201110-qbb','route_qm10-qbb-study','route_qm12-201110-qbb','route_qm12-qbb-study','route_qm18-201110-qbb','route_qm18-qbb-study') and record_kind='route';"
record_existence_and_kind.result_summary: 16/16 selected alias and target records exist as record_kind=route. Route IDs and service variants match within each pair: Bx41/local, Bx55/limited_stop, BxM6/express, BxM7/express, Q101/local, QM10/express, QM12/express, and QM18/express.

existing_alias_conflicts.merges_json_result: no output; none of the proposed aliases are already present in data/identity-overrides/merges.json
existing_alias_conflicts.identity_aliases_command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select kind, alias, target from identity_aliases where kind='route' and alias in ('route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster','route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster','route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_q101-201110-qbb','route_qm10-201110-qbb','route_qm12-201110-qbb','route_qm18-201110-qbb');"
existing_alias_conflicts.identity_aliases_result: no output; none of the proposed aliases are already present in canonical identity_aliases
existing_alias_conflicts.target_alias_guard_command: jq -r '.aliases.route as $r | ["route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1","route_bx55-limited-webster-2012","route_bxm6","route_bxm7","route_q101-qbb-study","route_qm10-qbb-study","route_qm12-qbb-study","route_qm18-qbb-study"][] | select($r[.] != null) | . + " -> " + $r[.]' data/identity-overrides/merges.json && sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select kind, alias, target from identity_aliases where kind='route' and alias in ('route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1','route_bx55-limited-webster-2012','route_bxm6','route_bxm7','route_q101-qbb-study','route_qm10-qbb-study','route_qm12-qbb-study','route_qm18-qbb-study');"
existing_alias_conflicts.target_alias_guard_result: no output from both checks; selected targets are not themselves registered aliases

do_not_merge_conflicts.db_command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "with pairs(alias,target) as (values ('route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster','route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1'),('route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster','route_bx55-limited-webster-2012'),('route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm6'),('route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025','route_bxm7'),('route_q101-201110-qbb','route_q101-qbb-study'),('route_qm10-201110-qbb','route_qm10-qbb-study'),('route_qm12-201110-qbb','route_qm12-qbb-study'),('route_qm18-201110-qbb','route_qm18-qbb-study')) select d.* from do_not_merge d join pairs p on d.kind='route' and ((d.record_id_a=p.alias and d.record_id_b=p.target) or (d.record_id_a=p.target and d.record_id_b=p.alias));"
do_not_merge_conflicts.db_result: no output; no selected pair is present in canonical do_not_merge
do_not_merge_conflicts.json_command: jq -r '.pairs.route[]? | select((.record_ids | index("route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster") and index("route_bx41-local-2012-02-brt-webster-cac1")) or (.record_ids | index("route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster") and index("route_bx55-limited-webster-2012")) or (.record_ids | index("route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025") and index("route_bxm6")) or (.record_ids | index("route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025") and index("route_bxm7")) or (.record_ids | index("route_q101-201110-qbb") and index("route_q101-qbb-study")) or (.record_ids | index("route_qm10-201110-qbb") and index("route_qm10-qbb-study")) or (.record_ids | index("route_qm12-201110-qbb") and index("route_qm12-qbb-study")) or (.record_ids | index("route_qm18-201110-qbb") and index("route_qm18-qbb-study"))) | .record_ids | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json
do_not_merge_conflicts.json_result: no output; no selected pair is present in data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json

prior_prep_conflicts_through_identity105.command: for f in data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch0*-prep.json data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch10[0-5]-prep.json; do jq -r --arg f "$f" '.proposed_aliases[]? | select(.kind=="route") | select(.alias_id as $a | ["route_bx41-local-2012-11-webster","route_bx55-limited-2012-11-webster","route_bxm6-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025","route_bxm7-madison-ave-cb6-jun2025","route_q101-201110-qbb","route_qm10-201110-qbb","route_qm12-201110-qbb","route_qm18-201110-qbb"] | index($a)) | [$f,.alias_id,.target_id] | @tsv' "$f" 2>/dev/null; done
prior_prep_conflicts_through_identity105.result: no output; none of the proposed aliases appear in prior codex identity prep artifacts through batch105

source_evidence_spot_check.result_summary: Selected records carry source evidence for the same route labels and compatible route types: Bx41 Local, Bx55 Limited, BxM6/BxM7 express, Q101 local, and QM10/QM12/QM18 express service.

forbidden_command_compliance.result: No external/provider-backed commands or profiles/providers were used. No canonicalize, canonicalize-wave, canonicalize-review, campaign dedup, identity-review-run, write, ask, schema-propose, ontology-normalize-run, materialize, export-jsonl, or validate commands were run. Inspection used local deterministic commands only: pwd, git status, ls, sed/head, rg, jq, sqlite3, and date; the only write was this prep artifact.
